titleStmt.title: SII 26.6: original edition by P. R. Srinivasan
No. 6. (A.R. No. 6 of 1909.) POṈ-AMARĀVATI, TIRUMAYAM TALUK, TIRUCHIRAPPALLI DISTRICT.

summary.p: On the south wall of the central shrine in the Chōḷēśvara temple.

p.hi: Sundarapāṇḍyadēva: (Māṟº Sundº Pāṇḍya I) Year 11+1 = 1227-28 A.D.

summary.p: The king is described here by the title Śōṇāḍu-vaḻaṅgiyaruḷiya.

It records that the group of people called the Maṟamāṇikkar of Poṉṉamarāpati in Puṟamalai-nāḍu and other districts conferred the title of Maṟachchakravarti-piḷḷai on Tiruvaraṉkuḷa-muḍaiyāṉ as a reward for his reciting the Pēr-vañji, and also granted some tax-free lands for reciting verses in praise of Perumāḷ of Poṉṉamarāpati.
Cf. Inscriptions (Texts) of the Pudukkottai State, No. 278 where a fuller text with another part is given. According to this publication the inscription is found in the Vishṇu temple.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svastiśrī . coṇāṭu vaḻaṅkiyaruḷiya cuntarapāṇṭiyatevaṟku yāṇṭu patiṉoṉṟāvatiṉetirāmāṇṭu puṟamalaiṉāṭṭup poṉṉamarāpati mutalāṉa ṉāṭukaḷil maṟamā
ṇikkarom pervañci keṭṭup paricalāka kuṭuttavan tiruvaraṉkuḷamuṭaiyāṉukku maṟaccakkaravattippiḷḷaiyeṉṟu peruṅkuṭuttu tūttikkuṭi vayalil vaṭapākam viḷainilamum Itukkel
laiyāṉa puṉceykaḷum muṉpu Eḻuti Aṇintu kuṭutta cempiṉpaṭi cantirātittavarai Iṟaiyiliye kāṇiyākak kuṭuttom Itukkuppoṉṉamarāpatippe
rumāḷ tiruṉāmattum Iyal viṇṇappañceyvārākavum tāṉvari Eppeṟpaṭṭaṉavum Iṟukkakkaṭavatallavāka Ippaṭi cammatittuk kalveṭṭikkuṭuttom
[panmāheśva*] rar rakṣai . Itil tūtti ṭiyil naṉcey puṉcey Uḷḷavai Aṭaṅkalum tevaṉ tiruvaraṉA fuller text is given in Pudu. Ins. No. 278. A fresh copy of the inscription has to be obtained.
Digital edition of SII 26.6 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
